articles.headline: Parliament Passes VB G RAM G Bill, Replaces MGNREGA Amid Opposition Boycott
articles.explainer:
• The Parliament of India has passed the 'Village Bharat Gramin Rozgar Abhiyan Mission Gramin' (VB G RAM G) bill.
• This new legislation is set to replace the Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act (MGNREGA).
• The bill was passed amidst a significant boycott by opposition parties, raising concerns about parliamentary procedure and democratic debate.
• The government asserts the new bill aims to streamline rural employment schemes, enhance skill development, and ensure more targeted assistance.
• Critics argue the new bill may dilute the universal right to work aspect and reduce the scope of guaranteed employment.
gsSubTopics: Government Policies and Interventions for Development in various sectors
Welfare Schemes for Vulnerable Sections
Statutory, Regulatory and various Quasi-Judicial Bodies
Parliament and State Legislatures – structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these
prelimsPoints: VB G RAM G: Village Bharat Gramin Rozgar Abhiyan Mission Gramin.
Replaces MGNREGA: Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act.
MGNREGA was enacted in 2005, guaranteeing 100 days of wage employment in a financial year to adult members of any rural household willing to do unskilled manual work.
VB G RAM G aims for targeted skill development and infrastructure creation.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the potential impact of the VB G RAM G bill on rural livelihoods and socio-economic equality, comparing its provisions with those of MGNREGA.
Discuss the implications of parliamentary boycotts on the legislative process and democratic accountability in India.
Examine the shift in approach from universal entitlement (MGNREGA) to targeted intervention (VB G RAM G) in India's welfare policy framework.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of the Parliament of India building with a bill document in the foreground.
articles.trivia: MGNREGA is considered one of the largest social welfare programmes in the world, aiming to address poverty and rural distress.

articles.headline: India and Oman Ink Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement (CEPA)
articles.explainer:
• India and Oman have officially signed a Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement (CEPA) in a landmark move to boost bilateral trade and investment.
• The agreement covers a wide range of sectors, including goods, services, investment, and digital trade, aiming to reduce tariffs and non-tariff barriers.
• This CEPA is expected to significantly enhance economic cooperation, facilitate market access for Indian goods and services in Oman, and vice-versa.
• The agreement is also seen as a strategic step to strengthen India's presence in the Gulf region and diversify its trade partnerships.
• Experts predict a substantial increase in bilateral trade volume and new avenues for joint ventures and technological collaboration.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: CEPA: Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement.
Oman is a key strategic partner for India in the Gulf region.
The agreement aims to boost trade in goods and services, and investment flows.
India's major exports to Oman include petroleum products, iron and steel, electronics.
Major imports from Oman include petroleum, fertilizers, minerals.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic and economic significance of the India-Oman CEPA for India's 'Look West' policy and energy security.
Discuss how CEPA agreements contribute to global trade liberalization and what challenges India faces in negotiating such pacts.
Evaluate the potential impact of the CEPA on key Indian sectors such as petroleum, IT services, and agriculture, considering both opportunities and challenges.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and Oman side-by-side with a handshake overlay.
articles.trivia: Oman's port of Duqm is strategically important, offering India access to the Arabian Sea and beyond, bypassing the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.headline: Rioting Erupts at Kolkata's Salt Lake Stadium After Messi's Abrupt Exit
articles.explainer:
• Rioting broke out among agitated fans at the Salt Lake Stadium in Kolkata following an appearance by football superstar Lionel Messi.
• The disturbance occurred after Messi's appearance was abruptly ended, leading to widespread disappointment and anger among the massive crowd.
• Police and security forces struggled to control the situation as fans clashed with personnel and damaged stadium property.
• Several injuries were reported, and authorities have launched an investigation into the security lapses and the cause of the sudden termination of Messi's event.
• The incident highlights challenges in crowd management and event organization for high-profile international celebrity appearances in India.
gsSubTopics: Social empowerment
Role of women and women’s organization, population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
Law and Order
prelimsPoints: Salt Lake Stadium (Vivekananda Yuba Bharati Krirangan) is located in Kolkata, West Bengal.
It is one of the largest multi-purpose stadiums in India, primarily used for football.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors contributing to public disorder during mass gatherings and suggest measures for effective crowd management and event security in India.
Discuss the role of local administration and event organizers in ensuring public safety during high-profile events, drawing lessons from the Salt Lake Stadium incident.
Examine the socio-psychological aspects that lead to fan violence and rioting, especially concerning celebrity events.
articles.imageDescription: Image of a large crowd in a stadium, with some commotion visible.
articles.trivia: Kolkata is often referred to as the 'Mecca of Indian Football' due to its passionate football culture and historical clubs.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Advanced Ocean Monitoring Satellite 'Samudra-3'
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) has successfully launched 'Samudra-3', an advanced Earth observation satellite dedicated to ocean monitoring.
• Launched from the Satish Dhawan Space Centre, Sriharikota, the satellite aims to provide high-resolution data for oceanography, marine meteorology, and coastal zone management.
• Samudra-3 will enhance India's capabilities in predicting ocean states, tracking marine biodiversity, and aiding disaster management related to cyclones and tsunamis.
• This mission reinforces India's commitment to leveraging space technology for sustainable development and environmental protection.
• The data from Samudra-3 is expected to benefit various sectors including fishing, shipping, climate research, and coastal security.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space
Environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
prelimsPoints: Samudra-3 is an Earth Observation Satellite.
Its primary purpose is ocean monitoring.
Launched by ISRO (Indian Space Research Organisation) from Satish Dhawan Space Centre, Sriharikota.
Provides data for marine meteorology, ocean state forecasting, and disaster management (cyclones, tsunamis).
Follows previous 'Samudra' series missions.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of advanced ocean monitoring satellites like Samudra-3 for India's economic and strategic interests, particularly in the context of the Blue Economy.
Analyze how ISRO's indigenous space capabilities contribute to India's scientific advancements and its role in global climate change mitigation and adaptation efforts.
Examine the applications of geospatial technology and remote sensing in various sectors, including disaster management, resource mapping, and urban planning.
articles.imageDescription: A rocket launching into space from a launchpad.
articles.trivia: ISRO's first dedicated ocean observation satellite, Oceansat-1, was launched in 1999.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Directs Centre to Expedite National Data Governance Policy Implementation
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India has issued a directive to the Central Government, urging the expeditious implementation of the National Data Governance Policy.
• The directive came during a hearing on a petition concerning data privacy and the ethical use of public data.
• The court emphasized the need for a robust legal framework to protect citizens' data, ensure transparency, and prevent misuse in the digital age.
• The National Data Governance Policy aims to standardize data collection, storage, and access across government entities and promote data sharing for public good while ensuring privacy.
• This ruling underscores the judiciary's role in shaping India's digital future and safeguarding fundamental rights in the evolving technological landscape.
gsSubTopics: Government Policies and Interventions for Development in various sectors
Role of Civil Services in a Democracy.
Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions.
prelimsPoints: National Data Governance Policy aims to standardize data access and use across government.
The Supreme Court's directive emphasizes data privacy and protection.
Justice K.S. Puttaswamy (Retd.) vs Union of India established the Right to Privacy as a fundamental right under Article 21.
Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act) is India's key legislation in this area.
mainsPoints: Discuss the critical need for a comprehensive National Data Governance Policy in India, considering the balance between data utilization for public good and individual privacy rights.
Analyze the role of the judiciary in ensuring the effective implementation of government policies and safeguarding fundamental rights in the context of emerging technologies.

Examine the challenges in establishing a robust data protection framework in a digitally transforming economy like India, including issues of enforcement, technological advancements, and public awareness.
articles.imageDescription: Supreme Court of India building facade.
articles.trivia: India is home to one of the largest digital user bases globally, making data governance a critical national priority.

articles.headline: G20 Sherpa Meeting Concludes in Delhi, Focus on Sustainable Development Goals
articles.explainer:
• The G20 Sherpa meeting concluded in New Delhi, laying the groundwork for the upcoming Leaders' Summit.
• Discussions primarily focused on accelerating progress towards the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), global economic stability, and climate finance.
• India, as the current host, emphasized the need for collective action on food security, energy transition, and digital public infrastructure.
• Sherpas from member countries engaged in intense negotiations to finalize the draft communiqué, addressing geopolitical tensions and their economic ramifications.
• The meeting highlighted India's continued leadership in shaping the global agenda and fostering multilateral cooperation on critical issues.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
prelimsPoints: G20 (Group of Twenty) is an intergovernmental forum comprising 19 countries and the European Union (EU).
It works to address major issues related to the global economy, such as international financial stability, climate change mitigation, and sustainable development.
Sherpas are personal representatives of the leaders of G20 countries, responsible for preparing the agenda and negotiating the communiqué for the summit.
SDGs (Sustainable Development Goals) are a collection of 17 interlinked global goals designed to be a 'blueprint to achieve a better and more sustainable future for all'.
mainsPoints: Evaluate India's role and contributions as the host of the G20 Sherpa meeting in advancing the agenda on sustainable development and global economic recovery.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities for multilateral forums like the G20 in addressing complex global issues such as climate change, geopolitical conflicts, and economic disparities.
Analyze the importance of digital public infrastructure (DPI) as a key agenda item for India in global forums and its potential to drive inclusive growth.
articles.imageDescription: Delegates seated around a large conference table with G20 logo.
articles.trivia: The G20 represents around two-thirds of the world's population, 85% of global GDP, and 75% of global trade.

articles.headline: Union Government Unveils 'Arogya Bharat' Comprehensive Health Policy
articles.explainer:
• The Union Government has launched 'Arogya Bharat', a new comprehensive national health policy aimed at universal health coverage and preventive healthcare.
• The policy focuses on strengthening primary healthcare infrastructure, expanding health insurance coverage, and promoting digital health solutions.
• Key initiatives include increased public health spending, establishment of more wellness centers, and a national campaign for lifestyle disease prevention.
• 'Arogya Bharat' seeks to address disparities in healthcare access and quality across urban and rural areas, with a special focus on vulnerable populations.
• The policy is envisioned as a long-term roadmap to achieve health equity and improve health outcomes for all citizens by 2035.
gsSubTopics: Government Policies and Interventions for Development in various sectors
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health
Welfare Schemes for Vulnerable Sections
articles.source: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
prelimsPoints: Arogya Bharat is a new comprehensive national health policy.
Aims for universal health coverage and preventive healthcare.
Focuses on primary healthcare, health insurance expansion, and digital health.
Previous major health policy in India was the National Health Policy 2017.
Ayushman Bharat Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (PMJAY) is a flagship health insurance scheme.
mainsPoints: Critically evaluate the 'Arogya Bharat' policy's potential to achieve universal health coverage in India, considering the challenges of funding, infrastructure, and human resources.
Discuss the role of preventive healthcare and digital health solutions in transforming India's public health landscape, as envisioned by the new policy.
Examine how the 'Arogya Bharat' policy addresses health disparities and what measures are needed for its effective implementation across diverse regions of India.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic depicting a healthy family with healthcare symbols.
articles.trivia: India's public health spending as a percentage of GDP has traditionally been among the lowest globally, a challenge the new policy aims to address.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Inflation Concerns
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) has decided to maintain the status quo on the key repo rate.
• The decision comes amidst persistent concerns over inflation, despite recent moderation, and a cautious outlook on global economic uncertainties.
• The MPC reiterated its commitment to aligning inflation with the target while supporting growth, maintaining an 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance.
• Analysts suggest the RBI is balancing the need to control inflation with supporting economic recovery, especially in the wake of global headwinds.
• The decision impacts lending rates for banks and consequently, borrowing costs for consumers and businesses, influencing overall economic activity.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Monetary Policy.
prelimsPoints: Repo rate is the rate at which the RBI lends money to commercial banks.
Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is a six-member body (3 from RBI, 3 appointed by GoI) responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate in India.
RBI's primary objective is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
Inflation target set by the government is 4% with a band of +/- 2%.
mainsPoints: Analyze the rationale behind the RBI's decision to maintain the status quo on the repo rate, considering the current economic landscape and global factors.
Discuss the challenges faced by the Monetary Policy Committee in balancing inflation control with economic growth objectives in a developing economy like India.
Examine the impact of the RBI's monetary policy stance on various sectors of the Indian economy, including banking, manufacturing, and consumer spending.
articles.imageDescription: Reserve Bank of India building with currency notes in the foreground.
articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee was established in 2016 to bring more transparency and accountability to monetary policy decisions.

articles.headline: India Launches 'Amrit Dharohar' Project for Wetlands Conservation
articles.explainer:
• India has launched the 'Amrit Dharohar' project, a national initiative focused on the conservation and sustainable management of its wetlands.
• The project aims to enhance the ecological character of wetlands, promote their wise use, and generate livelihood opportunities for local communities.
• It involves mapping, restoration, and monitoring of critical wetlands, with a special emphasis on Ramsar Sites and other ecologically significant areas.
• 'Amrit Dharohar' aligns with India's international commitments under the Ramsar Convention and its broader environmental protection agenda.
• The initiative is expected to boost biodiversity conservation, climate change mitigation, and ensure the ecological services provided by wetlands.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Disaster and disaster management.
articles.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: Amrit Dharohar is a project for wetlands conservation in India.
Ramsar Convention is an international treaty for the conservation and sustainable use of wetlands.
India has a significant number of Ramsar Sites, which are wetlands of international importance.
Wetlands provide critical ecosystem services like water purification, flood control, and habitat for biodiversity.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological and socio-economic significance of wetlands and evaluate the efficacy of initiatives like 'Amrit Dharohar' in achieving their conservation goals in India.
Analyze the challenges faced in wetlands conservation in India, including urbanization, pollution, and climate change, and suggest policy measures for their effective protection.
Examine India's international commitments under the Ramsar Convention and how domestic projects like 'Amrit Dharohar' contribute to fulfilling these obligations.
articles.imageDescription: A serene wetland landscape with birds and water.
articles.trivia: World Wetlands Day is celebrated annually on February 2nd, marking the date of the adoption of the Convention on Wetlands in 1971.

articles.headline: Government Unveils New Foreign Trade Policy 2025-30, Aims for $2 Trillion Exports
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Commerce and Industry has announced the new Foreign Trade Policy (FTP) 2025-30, targeting an ambitious $2 trillion in goods and services exports.
• The policy focuses on incentivizing domestic manufacturing, promoting e-commerce exports, and diversifying export markets beyond traditional partners.
• Key pillars include ease of doing business for exporters, district-level export hubs, and simplified procedures for MSMEs.
• The FTP aims to leverage India's growing digital infrastructure and logistical improvements to boost global competitiveness.
• This long-term policy framework is designed to make India a global trading hub and integrate it more deeply into global value chains.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Industrial Policy
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: Foreign Trade Policy (FTP) is a set of guidelines and instructions established by the Directorate General of Foreign Trade (DGFT) for the import and export of goods and services in India.
The new FTP 2025-30 aims for $2 trillion in exports.
Focuses on e-commerce exports and district-level export hubs.
MSMEs (Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises) are a key beneficiary group.
mainsPoints: Analyze the key objectives and strategies of the new Foreign Trade Policy 2025-30 and assess its potential to achieve the $2 trillion export target.
Discuss how the policy's emphasis on e-commerce exports and district-level hubs can contribute to inclusive economic growth and regional development in India.
Examine the challenges India faces in integrating into global value chains and how the new FTP addresses these issues to enhance India's global trade competitiveness.
articles.imageDescription: A container ship at a busy port, symbolizing international trade.
articles.trivia: India's first Foreign Trade Policy (then Export-Import Policy) was introduced in 1992.

articles.headline: India Unveils National Framework for AI Ethics and Governance
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) has released India's National Framework for Artificial Intelligence (AI) Ethics and Governance.
• The framework aims to ensure responsible and ethical development and deployment of AI technologies across various sectors in the country.
• It outlines principles such as fairness, transparency, accountability, safety, and privacy in AI systems, promoting 'AI for All' with a human-centric approach.
• The initiative positions India as a leader in establishing ethical guidelines for AI, balancing innovation with societal well-being.
• This framework will guide policymakers, industry, researchers, and civil society in fostering a trustworthy AI ecosystem.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Awareness in the fields of IT, Computers, Robotics, Nanotechnology, Bio-technology and issues relating to Intellectual Property Rights.
articles.source: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY)
prelimsPoints: National Framework for AI Ethics and Governance released by MeitY (Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology).
Key principles include fairness, transparency, accountability, safety, and privacy.
Aims to promote 'AI for All' with a human-centric approach.
India is a significant player in AI development and adoption.
mainsPoints: Discuss the critical need for a national framework on AI ethics and governance in India, considering the rapid advancements and potential societal impacts of Artificial Intelligence.
Analyze the key principles outlined in India's AI ethics framework and evaluate their effectiveness in ensuring responsible AI development and deployment.
Examine the challenges in implementing ethical AI guidelines across diverse sectors in India, including issues of data bias, explainability, and regulatory enforcement.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized brain with interconnected circuits, representing AI.
articles.trivia: India is one of the top countries globally in terms of AI skill penetration and adoption.

articles.headline: ASI Unearths Ancient Urban Settlement in Rajasthan, Reveals Trade Links
articles.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) has announced the discovery of a significant ancient urban settlement in Rajasthan.
• Preliminary excavations reveal evidence of a well-planned city, sophisticated water management systems, and artifacts indicating extensive trade links.
• The site, tentatively dated to the early historic period (circa 6th-4th century BCE), provides crucial insights into India's ancient urbanism and economic networks.
• Archaeologists have found pottery, seals, and tools suggesting connections with contemporary civilizations and trade routes.
• This discovery is expected to rewrite aspects of regional history and contribute significantly to understanding India's cultural heritage.
gsSubTopics: Indian Culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Archaeological sites
prelimsPoints: ASI (Archaeological Survey of India) is the premier organization for archaeological research and protection of cultural heritage in India.
Rajasthan has several significant historical sites, including those from the Indus Valley Civilization and various kingdoms.
Early Historic Period in India typically refers to the period from roughly 600 BCE to 300 CE, coinciding with the rise of Mahajanapadas and early empires.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of recent archaeological discoveries in understanding India's ancient urban planning, economic systems, and cultural interactions.
Examine the methods and challenges involved in archaeological excavations and how they contribute to reconstructing historical narratives.
Analyze the role of institutions like the ASI in preserving and promoting India's rich cultural heritage, highlighting the importance of public awareness and community involvement.
articles.imageDescription: Archaeologists excavating an ancient site in a desert-like region.
articles.trivia: Rajasthan is home to several UNESCO World Heritage Sites, including the Hill Forts of Rajasthan and Jaipur City.

articles.headline: Defence Acquisition Council Approves Major Indigenous Procurement Proposals
articles.explainer:
• The Defence Acquisition Council (DAC), chaired by the Defence Minister, has approved several capital acquisition proposals worth over ₹50,000 crore.
• The approvals prioritize indigenous design, development, and manufacturing of defence equipment under the 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' initiative.
• These procurements include advanced artillery systems, surveillance equipment, and upgrades for existing naval platforms.
• The move aims to bolster India's defence capabilities, reduce reliance on foreign imports, and boost the domestic defence industry.
• This decision is expected to create significant employment opportunities and foster technological self-reliance in the defence sector.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas
Defence technology
Indigenization of technology
prelimsPoints: Defence Acquisition Council (DAC) is the highest decision-making body in the Defence Ministry for new policy and capital acquisitions.
Chaired by the Defence Minister.
'Atmanirbhar Bharat' (Self-Reliant India) initiative aims to promote indigenous manufacturing.
India is among the top global defence importers, but is increasingly focusing on domestic production.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of indigenous defence production for India's national security and strategic autonomy, with reference to the 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' initiative.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities in strengthening India's domestic defence industrial base and reducing reliance on foreign defence imports.
Evaluate the role of the Defence Acquisition Council in streamlining the defence procurement process and promoting transparency and efficiency.
articles.imageDescription: Modern Indian military equipment, such as artillery or a naval ship.
articles.trivia: India has two Defence Industrial Corridors, one in Uttar Pradesh and another in Tamil Nadu, to boost defence manufacturing.

articles.headline: Government Launches 'Smart Cities 2.0' for Sustainable Urban Development
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs has unveiled 'Smart Cities 2.0', the next phase of India's urban development program.
• This phase focuses on integrating advanced technologies, green infrastructure, and citizen-centric services for sustainable and resilient cities.
• Key areas include smart mobility solutions, renewable energy integration, water management, and enhanced digital governance platforms.
• 'Smart Cities 2.0' aims to build upon the lessons learned from the first phase, emphasizing a more holistic and equitable approach to urban planning.
• The initiative is crucial for managing India's rapid urbanization and improving the quality of life for its growing urban population.
gsSubTopics: Urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
Role of women and women’s organization, population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues.
articles.source: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
prelimsPoints: Smart Cities Mission was launched in 2015 by the Government of India.
Aims to promote cities that provide core infrastructure, a decent quality of life to its citizens, a clean and sustainable environment, and application of 'Smart' solutions.
Smart Cities 2.0 focuses on advanced technologies, green infrastructure, and citizen-centric services.
Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs is the nodal ministry.
mainsPoints: Critically evaluate the 'Smart Cities 2.0' initiative's potential to address the multifaceted challenges of rapid urbanization in India, including infrastructure, environment, and social equity.
Discuss the role of technology and innovation in transforming Indian cities into sustainable and resilient urban centers, highlighting the importance of citizen participation.
Examine the lessons learned from the first phase of the Smart Cities Mission and how 'Smart Cities 2.0' aims to overcome previous limitations and achieve more holistic development.
articles.imageDescription: A futuristic city skyline with green spaces and smart technology elements.
articles.trivia: By 2050, India's urban population is projected to reach 814 million, making it one of the most urbanized countries globally.

articles.headline: India Unveils Enhanced National Cybersecurity Strategy 2025
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) has launched the enhanced National Cybersecurity Strategy 2025.
• The new strategy aims to bolster India's cyber defenses against evolving threats, protect critical information infrastructure, and foster a secure digital ecosystem.
• Key pillars include robust regulatory frameworks, capacity building, international cooperation, and promotion of indigenous cybersecurity products.
• The policy emphasizes a multi-stakeholder approach involving government, industry, academia, and citizens to create a resilient cyber environment.
• This updated strategy is crucial for safeguarding India's digital economy and national security in an increasingly interconnected world.
gsSubTopics: Cyber security
Basics of cyber security

Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security; money-laundering and its prevention.
prelimsPoints: National Cybersecurity Strategy 2025 launched by MeitY.
Aims to protect critical information infrastructure and foster a secure digital ecosystem.
CERT-In (Indian Computer Emergency Response Team) is the nodal agency for cybersecurity incidents in India.
Critical Information Infrastructure (CII) refers to assets vital for national security and economy, whose incapacitation would have a debilitating impact.
mainsPoints: Analyze the key features and objectives of India's enhanced National Cybersecurity Strategy 2025 and discuss its importance in safeguarding national security and the digital economy.
Examine the multifaceted challenges India faces in ensuring robust cybersecurity, including state-sponsored attacks, cybercrime, and data breaches.
Suggest measures for strengthening India's cybersecurity ecosystem, focusing on policy frameworks, technological advancements, international cooperation, and public awareness.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized digital lock with a map of India in the background.
articles.trivia: India's digital economy is projected to reach $1 trillion by 2025-26, making robust cybersecurity imperative.

articles.headline: ICAR Launches New Initiatives for Climate-Resilient Agriculture
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Council of Agricultural Research (ICAR) has launched a series of new initiatives to promote climate-resilient agriculture across India.
• These initiatives focus on developing drought-resistant crop varieties, precision farming techniques, and sustainable water management practices.
• The program aims to help farmers adapt to changing climate patterns, reduce crop losses, and ensure food security in the face of environmental challenges.
• ICAR will collaborate with state agricultural universities and farmers' organizations for research, development, and dissemination of these practices.
• This move is critical for sustaining agricultural productivity and farmer livelihoods in a country highly vulnerable to climate change impacts.
gsSubTopics: Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation systems storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.

Issues related to direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices; Public Distribution System- objectives, functioning, limitations, revamping; issues of buffer stocks and food security; Technology missions; economics of animal-rearing.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
prelimsPoints: ICAR (Indian Council of Agricultural Research) is an autonomous organization under the Department of Agricultural Research and Education (DARE), Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare.
It is the apex body for coordinating, guiding, and managing research and education in agriculture including horticulture, fisheries, and animal sciences in the entire country.
Climate-resilient agriculture aims to adapt farming practices to climate change and mitigate its effects.
Examples of climate-resilient practices include drip irrigation, conservation tillage, and diversification of crops.
mainsPoints: Discuss the imperative of transitioning to climate-resilient agriculture in India, considering the vulnerabilities of the agricultural sector to climate change.
Analyze the role of research institutions like ICAR in developing and disseminating innovative agricultural practices that enhance food security and farmer incomes.
Examine the policy and technological interventions required to scale up climate-resilient agriculture across diverse agro-climatic zones of India.
articles.imageDescription: A farmer working in a field with modern agricultural equipment.
articles.trivia: India is one of the world's largest agricultural producers, with agriculture contributing significantly to its GDP and employment.

articles.headline: India and Pakistan Hold Annual Review Meeting on Indus Waters Treaty
articles.explainer:
• India and Pakistan held their annual review meeting under the Permanent Indus Commission (PIC) to discuss various aspects of the Indus Waters Treaty.
• The talks focused on water sharing data, design objections to certain hydroelectric projects, and other technical issues concerning the shared river system.
• Despite ongoing political tensions, the treaty has largely endured as a framework for water cooperation between the two nuclear-armed neighbors.
• Both sides reiterated their commitment to upholding the provisions of the treaty and resolving differences through dialogue and established mechanisms.
• The meeting underscores the importance of the treaty as a critical confidence-building measure in the India-Pakistan relationship.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
prelimsPoints: Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) was signed in 1960 between India and Pakistan, brokered by the World Bank.
It allocates the waters of the Indus River System between the two countries.
Eastern Rivers (Ravi, Beas, Sutlej) are for India's unrestricted use.
Western Rivers (Indus, Jhelum, Chenab) are largely for Pakistan's unrestricted use, but India can use them for limited purposes like irrigation, power generation, and storage.
Permanent Indus Commission (PIC) is a bilateral commission established under the IWT.
mainsPoints: Analyze the enduring significance of the Indus Waters Treaty as a framework for water cooperation between India and Pakistan, despite geopolitical tensions.
Discuss the challenges and complexities involved in managing transboundary river systems, particularly in regions marked by historical conflicts.
Examine the potential implications of climate change on water resources in the Indus basin and how it might impact the future of the Indus Waters Treaty.
articles.imageDescription: A map showing the Indus River System and its tributaries.
articles.trivia: The Indus Waters Treaty is considered one of the most successful water-sharing treaties in the world, having survived multiple conflicts between India and Pakistan.

articles.headline: NCERT Unveils Revised School Curriculum Framework Aligned with NEP 2020
articles.explainer:
• The National Council of Educational Research and Training (NCERT) has unveiled its revised National Curriculum Framework (NCF) for school education.
• The new curriculum is fully aligned with the recommendations of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, focusing on holistic development, experiential learning, and critical thinking.
• Key changes include a multidisciplinary approach, reduced rote learning, emphasis on vocational education, and integration of Indian knowledge systems.
• The framework aims to prepare students for the 21st-century workforce while instilling cultural values and promoting creativity.
• Implementation will involve extensive teacher training and development of new textbooks and learning materials.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Education
Government Policies and Interventions for Development in various sectors
prelimsPoints: NCERT (National Council of Educational Research and Training) is an autonomous organization established in 1961 to assist and advise the Central and State Governments on policies and programmes for qualitative improvement in school education.
National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 aims for comprehensive reforms in India's education system from school to higher education.
The new NCF is aligned with the 5+3+3+4 curricular and pedagogical structure of NEP 2020.
mainsPoints: Analyze the key reforms proposed in the new National Curriculum Framework (NCF) and their potential to transform India's school education system in line with NEP 2020.
Discuss the challenges in implementing a holistic and multidisciplinary curriculum across diverse educational institutions in India, including teacher training and resource allocation.
Examine the significance of integrating Indian knowledge systems and vocational education into the mainstream curriculum for preparing students for future challenges and opportunities.
articles.imageDescription: Children studying in a modern classroom setting.
articles.trivia: The National Education Policy 2020 replaced the 1986 National Policy on Education, which had been in place for 34 years.

articles.headline: GST Council Recommends Rationalization of Tax Slabs, Boost for MSMEs
articles.explainer:
• The GST Council, in its latest meeting, has recommended a rationalization of Goods and Services Tax (GST) slabs and measures to boost MSMEs.
• The recommendations include simplifying compliance procedures, providing targeted relief for certain sectors, and potentially merging some existing tax slabs.
• The aim is to make the GST regime more efficient, reduce the burden on small businesses, and enhance tax collection while maintaining revenue neutrality.
• Finance Ministers from states and the Union discussed ways to improve the ease of doing business and address industry concerns regarding specific tax rates.
• These changes are expected to foster economic growth, improve the business environment, and streamline the indirect tax system.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it.
prelimsPoints: GST (Goods and Services Tax) is an indirect tax introduced in India on July 1, 2017.
GST Council is the governing body of GST, chaired by the Union Finance Minister, with state finance ministers as members.
Its recommendations are made after considering the views of both the Centre and states.
MSMEs (Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises) are crucial for India's economy and employment.
mainsPoints: Analyze the rationale behind the GST Council's recommendations for tax slab rationalization and their potential impact on economic efficiency and tax compliance in India.
Discuss the challenges faced by MSMEs under the current GST regime and evaluate how the proposed measures aim to provide relief and boost their growth.
Examine the concept of cooperative federalism as exemplified by the functioning of the GST Council in resolving complex fiscal issues between the Centre and states.
articles.imageDescription: A gavel on a table with Indian currency and GST logo.
articles.trivia: The GST Council's decisions require a three-fourths majority of the weighted votes of the members present and voting, with the Centre having one-third weightage and states two-thirds.

articles.headline: Border Roads Organisation Completes Strategic Tunnel in Himalayas
articles.explainer:
• The Border Roads Organisation (BRO) has announced the completion of a strategically vital all-weather tunnel in the Himalayan region.
• The tunnel will significantly improve connectivity to forward areas, reduce travel time, and ensure year-round access for military and civilian movement.
• This infrastructure project is crucial for enhancing India's defence preparedness along its northern borders and facilitating economic development in remote regions.
• The BRO utilized advanced engineering techniques to overcome challenging terrain and harsh weather conditions during construction.
• The completion marks a major milestone in India's ongoing efforts to upgrade border infrastructure and secure its territorial integrity.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: Border Roads Organisation (BRO) is a road construction executive force in India that constructs and maintains road networks in India's border areas and friendly neighboring countries.
It operates under the Ministry of Defence.
Himalayan region poses significant challenges for infrastructure development due to its rugged terrain and extreme weather.
Strategic tunnels like this enhance military logistics and civilian access.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of developing robust border infrastructure, such as all-weather tunnels, for India's national security and economic development in the Himalayan region.
Discuss the engineering challenges and environmental considerations involved in constructing infrastructure projects in high-altitude and ecologically sensitive border areas.
Examine the role of organizations like the BRO in strengthening India's connectivity and defence posture along its borders, highlighting their contributions to national integration.
articles.imageDescription: An entrance to a newly constructed tunnel in a mountainous landscape.
articles.trivia: The BRO has built over 60,000 km of roads and more than 650 bridges in India's border regions since its inception in 1960.

highlights: Parliament passes VB G RAM G Bill, replacing MGNREGA, amidst opposition boycott, sparking debate on rural employment policies.
India and Oman sign a Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement (CEPA) to boost bilateral trade and strengthen ties in the Gulf region.
ISRO successfully launches 'Samudra-3' satellite, enhancing India's capabilities in advanced ocean monitoring and climate research.
Supreme Court directs Central Government to expedite the implementation of the National Data Governance Policy, emphasizing data privacy and transparency.
G20 Sherpa meeting concludes in New Delhi, focusing on Sustainable Development Goals, climate finance, and global economic stability.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the VB G RAM G Bill, which recently replaced MGNREGA, consider the following statements:
1. The VB G RAM G Bill aims to provide universal guaranteed wage employment for 100 days to all rural households.
2. MGNREGA, which was replaced, was enacted in 2005.
3. The new bill emphasizes skill development and targeted assistance over universal entitlement.

Which of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect. The VB G RAM G Bill is stated to emphasize targeted assistance and skill development, potentially moving away from the universal guaranteed employment aspect of MGNREGA. MGNREGA itself guaranteed 100 days of wage employment. Statement 2 is correct; MGNREGA was indeed enacted in 2005. Statement 3 is correct; the explainer mentions that the new bill aims to streamline rural employment schemes, enhance skill development, and ensure more targeted assistance, indicating a shift from universal entitlement.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement (CEPA):
1. A CEPA typically covers trade in goods, services, and investment.
2. The recent India-Oman CEPA is India's first such agreement with a Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) member state.
3. CEPA aims to reduce tariffs and non-tariff barriers between signatory countries.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct; CEPA agreements are comprehensive and generally cover goods, services, and investment. Statement 3 is correct; the primary goal of CEPA and similar trade agreements is to reduce trade barriers. Statement 2 is incorrect; India has signed CEPA with UAE (a GCC member) in 2022, so the Oman CEPA is not the first with a GCC member.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to India's space technology and ocean monitoring, consider the following statements:
1. 'Samudra-3' is an Earth observation satellite launched by ISRO primarily for ocean monitoring.
2. The satellite will provide data useful for marine meteorology and disaster management related to cyclones.
3. ISRO's first dedicated ocean observation satellite was named Oceansat-1.

Which of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: All three statements are correct. 'Samudra-3' is indeed an advanced Earth observation satellite for ocean monitoring launched by ISRO. Its data is crucial for marine meteorology and disaster management, including cyclones. The trivia section confirms that Oceansat-1 was ISRO's first dedicated ocean observation satellite.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies is responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate in India?
options: (a) Reserve Bank of India Board of Directors
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is a statutory and institutionalized framework under the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934, for maintaining price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth. It is responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate (repo rate) in India.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following pairs:
1. Amrit Dharohar Project: Wetlands Conservation
2. National Cybersecurity Strategy 2025: Critical Information Infrastructure Protection
3. Arogya Bharat: Universal Basic Income

How many of the pairs given above are correctly matched?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Pair 1 is correctly matched: Amrit Dharohar Project is a national initiative for wetlands conservation. Pair 2 is correctly matched: The National Cybersecurity Strategy 2025 aims to bolster cyber defenses and protect critical information infrastructure. Pair 3 is incorrectly matched: Arogya Bharat is a comprehensive national health policy aimed at universal health coverage and preventive healthcare, not universal basic income.

mainsMocks.question: Critically analyze the potential implications of replacing MGNREGA with the VB G RAM G Bill on rural livelihoods, social safety nets, and the federal structure of India. What measures can be taken to ensure equitable and effective implementation of the new policy?
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Government Policies and Interventions for Development in various sectors; Welfare Schemes for Vulnerable Sections; Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the VB G RAM G Bill and its replacement of MGNREGA. Discuss the features and objectives of both. Analyze the 'potential implications' (positive and negative) on rural employment, income security, women's empowerment, migration, and local governance. Consider the shift from universal entitlement to targeted assistance. Discuss the federal implications of such a central legislation. Conclude with suggestions for equitable and effective implementation, including robust monitoring, transparency, and collaboration with states.

mainsMocks.question: The India-Oman Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement (CEPA) is a significant step in India's 'Look West' policy. Discuss the strategic and economic benefits of this agreement for India, particularly in the context of energy security and regional trade dynamics. What challenges might India face in maximizing the potential of such agreements?
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: India and its neighborhood- relations; Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests; Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the India-Oman CEPA and its context within India's 'Look West' policy. Detail the strategic benefits (e.g., access to critical waterways, security cooperation, diplomatic influence) and economic benefits (e.g., increased trade in goods/services, investment flows, energy imports, market diversification). Discuss how it impacts regional trade dynamics, especially with other GCC countries. Identify challenges such as geopolitical instability in the region, non-tariff barriers, competition from other trading blocs, and ensuring benefits trickle down to all sectors. Conclude with forward-looking measures.

mainsMocks.question: In an increasingly digital world, robust data governance and cybersecurity are paramount for national security and individual rights. Analyze India's efforts in this domain, with particular reference to the Supreme Court's directive on the National Data Governance Policy and the Enhanced National Cybersecurity Strategy 2025. What more needs to be done to build a secure and trustworthy digital ecosystem?
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Government Policies and Interventions for Development; Fundamental Rights; GS3: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life; Cyber security.
mainsMocks.approach: Begin by establishing the critical importance of data governance and cybersecurity. Discuss the Supreme Court's role and its directive for the National Data Governance Policy, highlighting its objectives (data protection, transparency, ethical use). Detail the key features and goals of the Enhanced National Cybersecurity Strategy 2025 (e.g., critical infrastructure protection, capacity building, international cooperation). Analyze how these initiatives address both national security concerns and individual rights (like the Right to Privacy). Conclude by suggesting further measures: stronger regulatory enforcement, public-private partnerships, international collaboration, skill development, public awareness campaigns, and continuous adaptation to emerging threats.

quickBytes.text: The VB G RAM G bill is set to replace the Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act (MGNREGA).
India and Oman signed a Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement (CEPA) to boost trade.
ISRO launched 'Samudra-3', an advanced Earth observation satellite for ocean monitoring.
The Supreme Court urged swift implementation of the National Data Governance Policy.
RBI's MPC maintained the repo rate status quo amidst inflation concerns.
The 'Amrit Dharohar' project was launched for wetlands conservation in India.
India unveiled the National Framework for AI Ethics and Governance.
The Indus Waters Treaty (1960) governs water sharing between India and Pakistan.
NCERT released a revised National Curriculum Framework aligned with NEP 2020.
The GST Council recommended rationalization of tax slabs and relief for MSMEs.

GS1.headline: Rioting Erupts at Kolkata's Salt Lake Stadium After Messi's Abrupt Exit
GS1.explainer:
• Rioting broke out among agitated fans at the Salt Lake Stadium in Kolkata following an appearance by football superstar Lionel Messi.
• The disturbance occurred after Messi's appearance was abruptly ended, leading to widespread disappointment and anger among the massive crowd.
• Police and security forces struggled to control the situation as fans clashed with personnel and damaged stadium property.
• Several injuries were reported, and authorities have launched an investigation into the security lapses and the cause of the sudden termination of Messi's event.
• The incident highlights challenges in crowd management and event organization for high-profile international celebrity appearances in India.
gsSubTopics: Social empowerment
Role of women and women’s organization, population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
Law and Order
prelimsPoints: Salt Lake Stadium (Vivekananda Yuba Bharati Krirangan) is located in Kolkata, West Bengal.
It is one of the largest multi-purpose stadiums in India, primarily used for football.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors contributing to public disorder during mass gatherings and suggest measures for effective crowd management and event security in India.
Discuss the role of local administration and event organizers in ensuring public safety during high-profile events, drawing lessons from the Salt Lake Stadium incident.
Examine the socio-psychological aspects that lead to fan violence and rioting, especially concerning celebrity events.
GS1.imageDescription: Image of a large crowd in a stadium, with some commotion visible.
GS1.trivia: Kolkata is often referred to as the 'Mecca of Indian Football' due to its passionate football culture and historical clubs.

GS1.headline: ASI Unearths Ancient Urban Settlement in Rajasthan, Reveals Trade Links
GS1.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) has announced the discovery of a significant ancient urban settlement in Rajasthan.
• Preliminary excavations reveal evidence of a well-planned city, sophisticated water management systems, and artifacts indicating extensive trade links.
• The site, tentatively dated to the early historic period (circa 6th-4th century BCE), provides crucial insights into India's ancient urbanism and economic networks.
• Archaeologists have found pottery, seals, and tools suggesting connections with contemporary civilizations and trade routes.
• This discovery is expected to rewrite aspects of regional history and contribute significantly to understanding India's cultural heritage.
gsSubTopics: Indian Culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Archaeological sites
prelimsPoints: ASI (Archaeological Survey of India) is the premier organization for archaeological research and protection of cultural heritage in India.
Rajasthan has several significant historical sites, including those from the Indus Valley Civilization and various kingdoms.
Early Historic Period in India typically refers to the period from roughly 600 BCE to 300 CE, coinciding with the rise of Mahajanapadas and early empires.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of recent archaeological discoveries in understanding India's ancient urban planning, economic systems, and cultural interactions.
Examine the methods and challenges involved in archaeological excavations and how they contribute to reconstructing historical narratives.
Analyze the role of institutions like the ASI in preserving and promoting India's rich cultural heritage, highlighting the importance of public awareness and community involvement.
GS1.imageDescription: Archaeologists excavating an ancient site in a desert-like region.
GS1.trivia: Rajasthan is home to several UNESCO World Heritage Sites, including the Hill Forts of Rajasthan and Jaipur City.

GS1.headline: Government Launches 'Smart Cities 2.0' for Sustainable Urban Development
GS1.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs has unveiled 'Smart Cities 2.0', the next phase of India's urban development program.
• This phase focuses on integrating advanced technologies, green infrastructure, and citizen-centric services for sustainable and resilient cities.
• Key areas include smart mobility solutions, renewable energy integration, water management, and enhanced digital governance platforms.
• 'Smart Cities 2.0' aims to build upon the lessons learned from the first phase, emphasizing a more holistic and equitable approach to urban planning.
• The initiative is crucial for managing India's rapid urbanization and improving the quality of life for its growing urban population.
gsSubTopics: Urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
Role of women and women’s organization, population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues.
GS1.source: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
prelimsPoints: Smart Cities Mission was launched in 2015 by the Government of India.
Aims to promote cities that provide core infrastructure, a decent quality of life to its citizens, a clean and sustainable environment, and application of 'Smart' solutions.
Smart Cities 2.0 focuses on advanced technologies, green infrastructure, and citizen-centric services.
Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs is the nodal ministry.
mainsPoints: Critically evaluate the 'Smart Cities 2.0' initiative's potential to address the multifaceted challenges of rapid urbanization in India, including infrastructure, environment, and social equity.
Discuss the role of technology and innovation in transforming Indian cities into sustainable and resilient urban centers, highlighting the importance of citizen participation.
Examine the lessons learned from the first phase of the Smart Cities Mission and how 'Smart Cities 2.0' aims to overcome previous limitations and achieve more holistic development.
GS1.imageDescription: A futuristic city skyline with green spaces and smart technology elements.
GS1.trivia: By 2050, India's urban population is projected to reach 814 million, making it one of the most urbanized countries globally.

GS2.headline: Parliament Passes VB G RAM G Bill, Replaces MGNREGA Amid Opposition Boycott
GS2.explainer:
• The Parliament of India has passed the 'Village Bharat Gramin Rozgar Abhiyan Mission Gramin' (VB G RAM G) bill.
• This new legislation is set to replace the Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act (MGNREGA).
• The bill was passed amidst a significant boycott by opposition parties, raising concerns about parliamentary procedure and democratic debate.
• The government asserts the new bill aims to streamline rural employment schemes, enhance skill development, and ensure more targeted assistance.
• Critics argue the new bill may dilute the universal right to work aspect and reduce the scope of guaranteed employment.
gsSubTopics: Government Policies and Interventions for Development in various sectors
Welfare Schemes for Vulnerable Sections
Statutory, Regulatory and various Quasi-Judicial Bodies
Parliament and State Legislatures – structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these
prelimsPoints: VB G RAM G: Village Bharat Gramin Rozgar Abhiyan Mission Gramin.
Replaces MGNREGA: Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act.
MGNREGA was enacted in 2005, guaranteeing 100 days of wage employment in a financial year to adult members of any rural household willing to do unskilled manual work.
VB G RAM G aims for targeted skill development and infrastructure creation.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the potential impact of the VB G RAM G bill on rural livelihoods and socio-economic equality, comparing its provisions with those of MGNREGA.
Discuss the implications of parliamentary boycotts on the legislative process and democratic accountability in India.
Examine the shift in approach from universal entitlement (MGNREGA) to targeted intervention (VB G RAM G) in India's welfare policy framework.
GS2.imageDescription: Illustration of the Parliament of India building with a bill document in the foreground.
GS2.trivia: MGNREGA is considered one of the largest social welfare programmes in the world, aiming to address poverty and rural distress.

GS2.headline: India and Oman Ink Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement (CEPA)
GS2.explainer:
• India and Oman have officially signed a Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement (CEPA) in a landmark move to boost bilateral trade and investment.
• The agreement covers a wide range of sectors, including goods, services, investment, and digital trade, aiming to reduce tariffs and non-tariff barriers.
• This CEPA is expected to significantly enhance economic cooperation, facilitate market access for Indian goods and services in Oman, and vice-versa.
• The agreement is also seen as a strategic step to strengthen India's presence in the Gulf region and diversify its trade partnerships.
• Experts predict a substantial increase in bilateral trade volume and new avenues for joint ventures and technological collaboration.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: CEPA: Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement.
Oman is a key strategic partner for India in the Gulf region.
The agreement aims to boost trade in goods and services, and investment flows.
India's major exports to Oman include petroleum products, iron and steel, electronics.
Major imports from Oman include petroleum, fertilizers, minerals.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic and economic significance of the India-Oman CEPA for India's 'Look West' policy and energy security.
Discuss how CEPA agreements contribute to global trade liberalization and what challenges India faces in negotiating such pacts.
Evaluate the potential impact of the CEPA on key Indian sectors such as petroleum, IT services, and agriculture, considering both opportunities and challenges.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and Oman side-by-side with a handshake overlay.
GS2.trivia: Oman's port of Duqm is strategically important, offering India access to the Arabian Sea and beyond, bypassing the Strait of Hormuz.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Directs Centre to Expedite National Data Governance Policy Implementation
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India has issued a directive to the Central Government, urging the expeditious implementation of the National Data Governance Policy.
• The directive came during a hearing on a petition concerning data privacy and the ethical use of public data.
• The court emphasized the need for a robust legal framework to protect citizens' data, ensure transparency, and prevent misuse in the digital age.
• The National Data Governance Policy aims to standardize data collection, storage, and access across government entities and promote data sharing for public good while ensuring privacy.
• This ruling underscores the judiciary's role in shaping India's digital future and safeguarding fundamental rights in the evolving technological landscape.
gsSubTopics: Government Policies and Interventions for Development in various sectors
Role of Civil Services in a Democracy.
Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions.
prelimsPoints: National Data Governance Policy aims to standardize data access and use across government.
The Supreme Court's directive emphasizes data privacy and protection.
Justice K.S. Puttaswamy (Retd.) vs Union of India established the Right to Privacy as a fundamental right under Article 21.
Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act) is India's key legislation in this area.
mainsPoints: Discuss the critical need for a comprehensive National Data Governance Policy in India, considering the balance between data utilization for public good and individual privacy rights.
Analyze the role of the judiciary in ensuring the effective implementation of government policies and safeguarding fundamental rights in the context of emerging technologies.

Examine the challenges in establishing a robust data protection framework in a digitally transforming economy like India, including issues of enforcement, technological advancements, and public awareness.
GS2.imageDescription: Supreme Court of India building facade.
GS2.trivia: India is home to one of the largest digital user bases globally, making data governance a critical national priority.

GS2.headline: G20 Sherpa Meeting Concludes in Delhi, Focus on Sustainable Development Goals
GS2.explainer:
• The G20 Sherpa meeting concluded in New Delhi, laying the groundwork for the upcoming Leaders' Summit.
• Discussions primarily focused on accelerating progress towards the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), global economic stability, and climate finance.
• India, as the current host, emphasized the need for collective action on food security, energy transition, and digital public infrastructure.
• Sherpas from member countries engaged in intense negotiations to finalize the draft communiqué, addressing geopolitical tensions and their economic ramifications.
• The meeting highlighted India's continued leadership in shaping the global agenda and fostering multilateral cooperation on critical issues.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
prelimsPoints: G20 (Group of Twenty) is an intergovernmental forum comprising 19 countries and the European Union (EU).
It works to address major issues related to the global economy, such as international financial stability, climate change mitigation, and sustainable development.
Sherpas are personal representatives of the leaders of G20 countries, responsible for preparing the agenda and negotiating the communiqué for the summit.
SDGs (Sustainable Development Goals) are a collection of 17 interlinked global goals designed to be a 'blueprint to achieve a better and more sustainable future for all'.
mainsPoints: Evaluate India's role and contributions as the host of the G20 Sherpa meeting in advancing the agenda on sustainable development and global economic recovery.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities for multilateral forums like the G20 in addressing complex global issues such as climate change, geopolitical conflicts, and economic disparities.
Analyze the importance of digital public infrastructure (DPI) as a key agenda item for India in global forums and its potential to drive inclusive growth.
GS2.imageDescription: Delegates seated around a large conference table with G20 logo.
GS2.trivia: The G20 represents around two-thirds of the world's population, 85% of global GDP, and 75% of global trade.

GS2.headline: Union Government Unveils 'Arogya Bharat' Comprehensive Health Policy
GS2.explainer:
• The Union Government has launched 'Arogya Bharat', a new comprehensive national health policy aimed at universal health coverage and preventive healthcare.
• The policy focuses on strengthening primary healthcare infrastructure, expanding health insurance coverage, and promoting digital health solutions.
• Key initiatives include increased public health spending, establishment of more wellness centers, and a national campaign for lifestyle disease prevention.
• 'Arogya Bharat' seeks to address disparities in healthcare access and quality across urban and rural areas, with a special focus on vulnerable populations.
• The policy is envisioned as a long-term roadmap to achieve health equity and improve health outcomes for all citizens by 2035.
gsSubTopics: Government Policies and Interventions for Development in various sectors
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health
Welfare Schemes for Vulnerable Sections
GS2.source: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
prelimsPoints: Arogya Bharat is a new comprehensive national health policy.
Aims for universal health coverage and preventive healthcare.
Focuses on primary healthcare, health insurance expansion, and digital health.
Previous major health policy in India was the National Health Policy 2017.
Ayushman Bharat Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (PMJAY) is a flagship health insurance scheme.
mainsPoints: Critically evaluate the 'Arogya Bharat' policy's potential to achieve universal health coverage in India, considering the challenges of funding, infrastructure, and human resources.
Discuss the role of preventive healthcare and digital health solutions in transforming India's public health landscape, as envisioned by the new policy.
Examine how the 'Arogya Bharat' policy addresses health disparities and what measures are needed for its effective implementation across diverse regions of India.
GS2.imageDescription: A graphic depicting a healthy family with healthcare symbols.
GS2.trivia: India's public health spending as a percentage of GDP has traditionally been among the lowest globally, a challenge the new policy aims to address.

GS2.headline: India and Pakistan Hold Annual Review Meeting on Indus Waters Treaty
GS2.explainer:
• India and Pakistan held their annual review meeting under the Permanent Indus Commission (PIC) to discuss various aspects of the Indus Waters Treaty.
• The talks focused on water sharing data, design objections to certain hydroelectric projects, and other technical issues concerning the shared river system.
• Despite ongoing political tensions, the treaty has largely endured as a framework for water cooperation between the two nuclear-armed neighbors.
• Both sides reiterated their commitment to upholding the provisions of the treaty and resolving differences through dialogue and established mechanisms.
• The meeting underscores the importance of the treaty as a critical confidence-building measure in the India-Pakistan relationship.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
prelimsPoints: Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) was signed in 1960 between India and Pakistan, brokered by the World Bank.
It allocates the waters of the Indus River System between the two countries.
Eastern Rivers (Ravi, Beas, Sutlej) are for India's unrestricted use.
Western Rivers (Indus, Jhelum, Chenab) are largely for Pakistan's unrestricted use, but India can use them for limited purposes like irrigation, power generation, and storage.
Permanent Indus Commission (PIC) is a bilateral commission established under the IWT.
mainsPoints: Analyze the enduring significance of the Indus Waters Treaty as a framework for water cooperation between India and Pakistan, despite geopolitical tensions.
Discuss the challenges and complexities involved in managing transboundary river systems, particularly in regions marked by historical conflicts.
Examine the potential implications of climate change on water resources in the Indus basin and how it might impact the future of the Indus Waters Treaty.
GS2.imageDescription: A map showing the Indus River System and its tributaries.
GS2.trivia: The Indus Waters Treaty is considered one of the most successful water-sharing treaties in the world, having survived multiple conflicts between India and Pakistan.

GS2.headline: NCERT Unveils Revised School Curriculum Framework Aligned with NEP 2020
GS2.explainer:
• The National Council of Educational Research and Training (NCERT) has unveiled its revised National Curriculum Framework (NCF) for school education.
• The new curriculum is fully aligned with the recommendations of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, focusing on holistic development, experiential learning, and critical thinking.
• Key changes include a multidisciplinary approach, reduced rote learning, emphasis on vocational education, and integration of Indian knowledge systems.
• The framework aims to prepare students for the 21st-century workforce while instilling cultural values and promoting creativity.
• Implementation will involve extensive teacher training and development of new textbooks and learning materials.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Education
Government Policies and Interventions for Development in various sectors
prelimsPoints: NCERT (National Council of Educational Research and Training) is an autonomous organization established in 1961 to assist and advise the Central and State Governments on policies and programmes for qualitative improvement in school education.
National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 aims for comprehensive reforms in India's education system from school to higher education.
The new NCF is aligned with the 5+3+3+4 curricular and pedagogical structure of NEP 2020.
mainsPoints: Analyze the key reforms proposed in the new National Curriculum Framework (NCF) and their potential to transform India's school education system in line with NEP 2020.
Discuss the challenges in implementing a holistic and multidisciplinary curriculum across diverse educational institutions in India, including teacher training and resource allocation.
Examine the significance of integrating Indian knowledge systems and vocational education into the mainstream curriculum for preparing students for future challenges and opportunities.
GS2.imageDescription: Children studying in a modern classroom setting.
GS2.trivia: The National Education Policy 2020 replaced the 1986 National Policy on Education, which had been in place for 34 years.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Advanced Ocean Monitoring Satellite 'Samudra-3'
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) has successfully launched 'Samudra-3', an advanced Earth observation satellite dedicated to ocean monitoring.
• Launched from the Satish Dhawan Space Centre, Sriharikota, the satellite aims to provide high-resolution data for oceanography, marine meteorology, and coastal zone management.
• Samudra-3 will enhance India's capabilities in predicting ocean states, tracking marine biodiversity, and aiding disaster management related to cyclones and tsunamis.
• This mission reinforces India's commitment to leveraging space technology for sustainable development and environmental protection.
• The data from Samudra-3 is expected to benefit various sectors including fishing, shipping, climate research, and coastal security.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space
Environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
prelimsPoints: Samudra-3 is an Earth Observation Satellite.
Its primary purpose is ocean monitoring.
Launched by ISRO (Indian Space Research Organisation) from Satish Dhawan Space Centre, Sriharikota.
Provides data for marine meteorology, ocean state forecasting, and disaster management (cyclones, tsunamis).
Follows previous 'Samudra' series missions.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of advanced ocean monitoring satellites like Samudra-3 for India's economic and strategic interests, particularly in the context of the Blue Economy.
Analyze how ISRO's indigenous space capabilities contribute to India's scientific advancements and its role in global climate change mitigation and adaptation efforts.
Examine the applications of geospatial technology and remote sensing in various sectors, including disaster management, resource mapping, and urban planning.
GS3.imageDescription: A rocket launching into space from a launchpad.
GS3.trivia: ISRO's first dedicated ocean observation satellite, Oceansat-1, was launched in 1999.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Inflation Concerns
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) has decided to maintain the status quo on the key repo rate.
• The decision comes amidst persistent concerns over inflation, despite recent moderation, and a cautious outlook on global economic uncertainties.
• The MPC reiterated its commitment to aligning inflation with the target while supporting growth, maintaining an 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance.
• Analysts suggest the RBI is balancing the need to control inflation with supporting economic recovery, especially in the wake of global headwinds.
• The decision impacts lending rates for banks and consequently, borrowing costs for consumers and businesses, influencing overall economic activity.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Monetary Policy.
prelimsPoints: Repo rate is the rate at which the RBI lends money to commercial banks.
Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is a six-member body (3 from RBI, 3 appointed by GoI) responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate in India.
RBI's primary objective is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
Inflation target set by the government is 4% with a band of +/- 2%.
mainsPoints: Analyze the rationale behind the RBI's decision to maintain the status quo on the repo rate, considering the current economic landscape and global factors.
Discuss the challenges faced by the Monetary Policy Committee in balancing inflation control with economic growth objectives in a developing economy like India.
Examine the impact of the RBI's monetary policy stance on various sectors of the Indian economy, including banking, manufacturing, and consumer spending.
