GS3.imageDescription: Reserve Bank of India building with currency notes in the foreground.
GS3.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee was established in 2016 to bring more transparency and accountability to monetary policy decisions.

GS3.headline: India Launches 'Amrit Dharohar' Project for Wetlands Conservation
GS3.explainer:
• India has launched the 'Amrit Dharohar' project, a national initiative focused on the conservation and sustainable management of its wetlands.
• The project aims to enhance the ecological character of wetlands, promote their wise use, and generate livelihood opportunities for local communities.
• It involves mapping, restoration, and monitoring of critical wetlands, with a special emphasis on Ramsar Sites and other ecologically significant areas.
• 'Amrit Dharohar' aligns with India's international commitments under the Ramsar Convention and its broader environmental protection agenda.
• The initiative is expected to boost biodiversity conservation, climate change mitigation, and ensure the ecological services provided by wetlands.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Disaster and disaster management.
GS3.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: Amrit Dharohar is a project for wetlands conservation in India.
Ramsar Convention is an international treaty for the conservation and sustainable use of wetlands.
India has a significant number of Ramsar Sites, which are wetlands of international importance.
Wetlands provide critical ecosystem services like water purification, flood control, and habitat for biodiversity.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological and socio-economic significance of wetlands and evaluate the efficacy of initiatives like 'Amrit Dharohar' in achieving their conservation goals in India.
Analyze the challenges faced in wetlands conservation in India, including urbanization, pollution, and climate change, and suggest policy measures for their effective protection.
Examine India's international commitments under the Ramsar Convention and how domestic projects like 'Amrit Dharohar' contribute to fulfilling these obligations.
GS3.imageDescription: A serene wetland landscape with birds and water.
GS3.trivia: World Wetlands Day is celebrated annually on February 2nd, marking the date of the adoption of the Convention on Wetlands in 1971.

GS3.headline: Government Unveils New Foreign Trade Policy 2025-30, Aims for $2 Trillion Exports
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Commerce and Industry has announced the new Foreign Trade Policy (FTP) 2025-30, targeting an ambitious $2 trillion in goods and services exports.
• The policy focuses on incentivizing domestic manufacturing, promoting e-commerce exports, and diversifying export markets beyond traditional partners.
• Key pillars include ease of doing business for exporters, district-level export hubs, and simplified procedures for MSMEs.
• The FTP aims to leverage India's growing digital infrastructure and logistical improvements to boost global competitiveness.
• This long-term policy framework is designed to make India a global trading hub and integrate it more deeply into global value chains.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Industrial Policy
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: Foreign Trade Policy (FTP) is a set of guidelines and instructions established by the Directorate General of Foreign Trade (DGFT) for the import and export of goods and services in India.
The new FTP 2025-30 aims for $2 trillion in exports.
Focuses on e-commerce exports and district-level export hubs.
MSMEs (Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises) are a key beneficiary group.
mainsPoints: Analyze the key objectives and strategies of the new Foreign Trade Policy 2025-30 and assess its potential to achieve the $2 trillion export target.
Discuss how the policy's emphasis on e-commerce exports and district-level hubs can contribute to inclusive economic growth and regional development in India.
Examine the challenges India faces in integrating into global value chains and how the new FTP addresses these issues to enhance India's global trade competitiveness.
GS3.imageDescription: A container ship at a busy port, symbolizing international trade.
GS3.trivia: India's first Foreign Trade Policy (then Export-Import Policy) was introduced in 1992.

GS3.headline: India Unveils National Framework for AI Ethics and Governance
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) has released India's National Framework for Artificial Intelligence (AI) Ethics and Governance.
• The framework aims to ensure responsible and ethical development and deployment of AI technologies across various sectors in the country.
• It outlines principles such as fairness, transparency, accountability, safety, and privacy in AI systems, promoting 'AI for All' with a human-centric approach.
• The initiative positions India as a leader in establishing ethical guidelines for AI, balancing innovation with societal well-being.
• This framework will guide policymakers, industry, researchers, and civil society in fostering a trustworthy AI ecosystem.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Awareness in the fields of IT, Computers, Robotics, Nanotechnology, Bio-technology and issues relating to Intellectual Property Rights.
GS3.source: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY)
prelimsPoints: National Framework for AI Ethics and Governance released by MeitY (Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology).
Key principles include fairness, transparency, accountability, safety, and privacy.
Aims to promote 'AI for All' with a human-centric approach.
India is a significant player in AI development and adoption.
mainsPoints: Discuss the critical need for a national framework on AI ethics and governance in India, considering the rapid advancements and potential societal impacts of Artificial Intelligence.
Analyze the key principles outlined in India's AI ethics framework and evaluate their effectiveness in ensuring responsible AI development and deployment.
Examine the challenges in implementing ethical AI guidelines across diverse sectors in India, including issues of data bias, explainability, and regulatory enforcement.
GS3.imageDescription: A stylized brain with interconnected circuits, representing AI.
GS3.trivia: India is one of the top countries globally in terms of AI skill penetration and adoption.

GS3.headline: Defence Acquisition Council Approves Major Indigenous Procurement Proposals
GS3.explainer:
• The Defence Acquisition Council (DAC), chaired by the Defence Minister, has approved several capital acquisition proposals worth over ₹50,000 crore.
• The approvals prioritize indigenous design, development, and manufacturing of defence equipment under the 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' initiative.
• These procurements include advanced artillery systems, surveillance equipment, and upgrades for existing naval platforms.
• The move aims to bolster India's defence capabilities, reduce reliance on foreign imports, and boost the domestic defence industry.
• This decision is expected to create significant employment opportunities and foster technological self-reliance in the defence sector.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas
Defence technology
Indigenization of technology
prelimsPoints: Defence Acquisition Council (DAC) is the highest decision-making body in the Defence Ministry for new policy and capital acquisitions.
Chaired by the Defence Minister.
'Atmanirbhar Bharat' (Self-Reliant India) initiative aims to promote indigenous manufacturing.
India is among the top global defence importers, but is increasingly focusing on domestic production.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of indigenous defence production for India's national security and strategic autonomy, with reference to the 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' initiative.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities in strengthening India's domestic defence industrial base and reducing reliance on foreign defence imports.
Evaluate the role of the Defence Acquisition Council in streamlining the defence procurement process and promoting transparency and efficiency.
GS3.imageDescription: Modern Indian military equipment, such as artillery or a naval ship.
GS3.trivia: India has two Defence Industrial Corridors, one in Uttar Pradesh and another in Tamil Nadu, to boost defence manufacturing.

GS3.headline: India Unveils Enhanced National Cybersecurity Strategy 2025
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) has launched the enhanced National Cybersecurity Strategy 2025.
• The new strategy aims to bolster India's cyber defenses against evolving threats, protect critical information infrastructure, and foster a secure digital ecosystem.
• Key pillars include robust regulatory frameworks, capacity building, international cooperation, and promotion of indigenous cybersecurity products.
• The policy emphasizes a multi-stakeholder approach involving government, industry, academia, and citizens to create a resilient cyber environment.
• This updated strategy is crucial for safeguarding India's digital economy and national security in an increasingly interconnected world.
gsSubTopics: Cyber security
Basics of cyber security

Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security; money-laundering and its prevention.
prelimsPoints: National Cybersecurity Strategy 2025 launched by MeitY.
Aims to protect critical information infrastructure and foster a secure digital ecosystem.
CERT-In (Indian Computer Emergency Response Team) is the nodal agency for cybersecurity incidents in India.
Critical Information Infrastructure (CII) refers to assets vital for national security and economy, whose incapacitation would have a debilitating impact.
mainsPoints: Analyze the key features and objectives of India's enhanced National Cybersecurity Strategy 2025 and discuss its importance in safeguarding national security and the digital economy.
Examine the multifaceted challenges India faces in ensuring robust cybersecurity, including state-sponsored attacks, cybercrime, and data breaches.
Suggest measures for strengthening India's cybersecurity ecosystem, focusing on policy frameworks, technological advancements, international cooperation, and public awareness.
GS3.imageDescription: A stylized digital lock with a map of India in the background.
GS3.trivia: India's digital economy is projected to reach $1 trillion by 2025-26, making robust cybersecurity imperative.

GS3.headline: ICAR Launches New Initiatives for Climate-Resilient Agriculture
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Council of Agricultural Research (ICAR) has launched a series of new initiatives to promote climate-resilient agriculture across India.
• These initiatives focus on developing drought-resistant crop varieties, precision farming techniques, and sustainable water management practices.
• The program aims to help farmers adapt to changing climate patterns, reduce crop losses, and ensure food security in the face of environmental challenges.
• ICAR will collaborate with state agricultural universities and farmers' organizations for research, development, and dissemination of these practices.
• This move is critical for sustaining agricultural productivity and farmer livelihoods in a country highly vulnerable to climate change impacts.
gsSubTopics: Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation systems storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.

Issues related to direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices; Public Distribution System- objectives, functioning, limitations, revamping; issues of buffer stocks and food security; Technology missions; economics of animal-rearing.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
prelimsPoints: ICAR (Indian Council of Agricultural Research) is an autonomous organization under the Department of Agricultural Research and Education (DARE), Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare.
It is the apex body for coordinating, guiding, and managing research and education in agriculture including horticulture, fisheries, and animal sciences in the entire country.
Climate-resilient agriculture aims to adapt farming practices to climate change and mitigate its effects.
Examples of climate-resilient practices include drip irrigation, conservation tillage, and diversification of crops.
mainsPoints: Discuss the imperative of transitioning to climate-resilient agriculture in India, considering the vulnerabilities of the agricultural sector to climate change.
Analyze the role of research institutions like ICAR in developing and disseminating innovative agricultural practices that enhance food security and farmer incomes.
Examine the policy and technological interventions required to scale up climate-resilient agriculture across diverse agro-climatic zones of India.
GS3.imageDescription: A farmer working in a field with modern agricultural equipment.
GS3.trivia: India is one of the world's largest agricultural producers, with agriculture contributing significantly to its GDP and employment.

GS3.headline: GST Council Recommends Rationalization of Tax Slabs, Boost for MSMEs
GS3.explainer:
• The GST Council, in its latest meeting, has recommended a rationalization of Goods and Services Tax (GST) slabs and measures to boost MSMEs.
• The recommendations include simplifying compliance procedures, providing targeted relief for certain sectors, and potentially merging some existing tax slabs.
• The aim is to make the GST regime more efficient, reduce the burden on small businesses, and enhance tax collection while maintaining revenue neutrality.
• Finance Ministers from states and the Union discussed ways to improve the ease of doing business and address industry concerns regarding specific tax rates.
• These changes are expected to foster economic growth, improve the business environment, and streamline the indirect tax system.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it.
prelimsPoints: GST (Goods and Services Tax) is an indirect tax introduced in India on July 1, 2017.
GST Council is the governing body of GST, chaired by the Union Finance Minister, with state finance ministers as members.
Its recommendations are made after considering the views of both the Centre and states.
MSMEs (Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises) are crucial for India's economy and employment.
mainsPoints: Analyze the rationale behind the GST Council's recommendations for tax slab rationalization and their potential impact on economic efficiency and tax compliance in India.
Discuss the challenges faced by MSMEs under the current GST regime and evaluate how the proposed measures aim to provide relief and boost their growth.
Examine the concept of cooperative federalism as exemplified by the functioning of the GST Council in resolving complex fiscal issues between the Centre and states.
GS3.imageDescription: A gavel on a table with Indian currency and GST logo.
GS3.trivia: The GST Council's decisions require a three-fourths majority of the weighted votes of the members present and voting, with the Centre having one-third weightage and states two-thirds.

GS3.headline: Border Roads Organisation Completes Strategic Tunnel in Himalayas
GS3.explainer:
• The Border Roads Organisation (BRO) has announced the completion of a strategically vital all-weather tunnel in the Himalayan region.
• The tunnel will significantly improve connectivity to forward areas, reduce travel time, and ensure year-round access for military and civilian movement.
• This infrastructure project is crucial for enhancing India's defence preparedness along its northern borders and facilitating economic development in remote regions.
• The BRO utilized advanced engineering techniques to overcome challenging terrain and harsh weather conditions during construction.
• The completion marks a major milestone in India's ongoing efforts to upgrade border infrastructure and secure its territorial integrity.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: Border Roads Organisation (BRO) is a road construction executive force in India that constructs and maintains road networks in India's border areas and friendly neighboring countries.
It operates under the Ministry of Defence.
Himalayan region poses significant challenges for infrastructure development due to its rugged terrain and extreme weather.
Strategic tunnels like this enhance military logistics and civilian access.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of developing robust border infrastructure, such as all-weather tunnels, for India's national security and economic development in the Himalayan region.
Discuss the engineering challenges and environmental considerations involved in constructing infrastructure projects in high-altitude and ecologically sensitive border areas.
Examine the role of organizations like the BRO in strengthening India's connectivity and defence posture along its borders, highlighting their contributions to national integration.
GS3.imageDescription: An entrance to a newly constructed tunnel in a mountainous landscape.
GS3.trivia: The BRO has built over 60,000 km of roads and more than 650 bridges in India's border regions since its inception in 1960.
